moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and stability, a core conservative holding.
Add XLK at 10% as a top-quality tech name with a strong scanner setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation, favoring income and broad index exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation and low drawdown. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and diversifying holdings, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with risk-first growth.
Increase QQQI to 25% to blend income and stability into the portfolio.
Add XLK at 10% and SMH at 15% as quality tech and semiconductor names with strong scanner setups.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a diversified approach.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth with moderate exposure. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index, quality tech, and income for stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and allow for higher growth allocations.
Sell SOXL to exit a losing leveraged ETF, adhering to strict stop discipline.
Trim QQQI to 15% to shift focus from income towards higher growth potential.
Increase SMH to 20% and add XLK at 15% to lean into strong semiconductor and tech setups.
Add META at 10% as a high-scoring AI/Software setup to further concentrate in growth areas.
This plan aggressively leans into high-scoring setups and allows for increased concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth with higher risk. It concentrates in high-scoring setups and uses leverage, which amplifies both potential gains and drawdowns, and leveraged ETFs decay daily.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reallocate for income.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD and trim SMH to 5% to reduce high-beta single name exposure, prioritizing stability.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize the income focus of the portfolio.
Trim QQQ to 10% to shift allocation from growth towards income-generating assets.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield with low drawdown. It prioritizes income ETFs and broad index exposure, significantly reducing growth potential and high-beta risk. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall.
